Patch Set 5:

I accidentally left in the oo_nr_writes check in the previous version.

But that probably doesn't explain the crash - I'm digging in to it now.

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 5:

Looking at this, I think the problem is - sort of - with the assertion...  There are (without this patch) just two lists the extent should be on, right?  oo_urgent_exts and oo_hp_exts.  The oo_urgent_exts case is taken care of because we "wait" for the extent if it's in that state.

Then there's the hp_exts case.  I'm having a harder time seeing how we avoid that completely, but I do see in osc_cache_writeback_range, where we put things on the oo_hp_exts list that in that case, we call osc_cache_wait_range, which takes care of it.

So maybe there's some protection or other I'm not aware of...  But I think this truncate code just assumes that extents which are not urgent won't be on a list.  It's not a hard requirement, it's just something it assumes and checks.  It wouldn't hurt anything to remove the extent from the full_exts list.

I'm going to try to reproduce the bug and test switching from asserting list_empty to checking...

Patch Set 7:

Hi Patrick, can you please take a look at LU-8135? That problem is similar to this one but we need to limit how many chunks that an RPC can cover. For example, if ZFS uses 1MB bock size and RPC size is set to 16MB, then one RPC can span (16MB/1MB)=16 chunks at most.

parents.subject: LU-8491 quota: sleep while holding spinlock

commit.subject: LU-8515 osc: Send RPCs when extents are full
commit.message:
LU-8515 osc: Send RPCs when extents are full

Currently, Lustre decides to send an RPC under a number of
conditions (such as memory pressure or lock cancellcation);
one of the conditions it looks for is "enough dirty pages
to fill an RPC". This worked fine when only one process
could be dirtying pages at a time, but in newer Lustre
versions, more than one process can write to the same
file (and the same osc object) at once.

In this case, the "count dirty pages method" will see there
are enough dirty pages to fill an RPC, but since the dirty
pages are being created by multiple writers, they are not
contiguous and will not fit in to one RPC. This resulted in
many RPCs of less than full size being sent, despite a
good I/O pattern. (Earlier versions of Lustre usually
send only full RPCs when presented with this pattern.)

Instead, we remove this check and add extents to a special
full extent list when they reach max pages per RPC, then
send from that list. (This is similar to high priority
and urgent extents.)

With a good I/O pattern, like usually used in benchmarking,
it should be possible to send only full size RPCs. This
patch achieves that without degrading performance in other
cases.

In IOR tests with multiple writers to a single file,
this patch improves performance by several times, and
returns performance to equal levels (single striped files)
or much greater levels (very high speed OSTs, files
with many stripes) vs earlier versions.

Supporting data is provided in LU-8515.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
Change-Id: Icc3c99b953d11f774600996648c9b20ed1f81f89
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/22012
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andrew Perepechko <andrew.perepechko@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
